extra.message: User input from data['name'] is concatenated directly into SQL string using string formatting (%) operator. This allows SQL injection attacks. The query should use parameterized queries with ? or %s placeholders and pass parameters separately.
MD5 hashing algorithm is used for password verification. MD5 is cryptographically broken and should not be used for password hashing. Use bcrypt, PBKDF2, scrypt, or argon2 instead.

cwe: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

extra.message: CSRF middleware is commented out (line 27: '# csrf_middleware,'). This disables CSRF token validation for POST requests, leaving the application vulnerable to Cross-Site Request Forgery attacks.

Jinja2 template autoescape is disabled (autoescape=False). This allows user-controlled data rendered in templates to execute as HTML/JavaScript, enabling Stored and Reflected XSS attacks. Autoescape should be set to True.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The evaluate endpoint (line 135) does not enforce authorization checks. Any authenticated user can create marks for any student/course combination. Only admin users should be able to evaluate students. The @authorize(ensure_admin=True) decorator should be applied.

Review text is rendered without escaping ({{ review.review_text }}) when Jinja2 autoescape is disabled. User-supplied review_text can contain HTML/JavaScript that will be executed. User input from the review endpoint (views.py:121) is stored in the database and displayed here without sanitization.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: Course description is rendered without escaping ({{ course.description }}) when Jinja2 autoescape is disabled. User-supplied course descriptions from views.py:90 can contain HTML/JavaScript that will be executed.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The students endpoint (line 52) allows any authenticated user to create new students via POST request. The @authorize(ensure_admin=True) decorator should be applied to restrict student creation to admin users only.

The courses endpoint (line 84) allows any authenticated user to create new courses via POST request. The @authorize(ensure_admin=True) decorator should be applied to restrict course creation to admin users only.
